content.problemStatement:
**This is an interactive problem.**

A mysterious organisation has stolen an EMP chip prototype from a high-tech US facility. IGI knows the consequences of the chip falling into the wrong hands. Since it is just a prototype, they wish to destroy it completely instead of attempting to retrieve it. The best opportunity to do so is when the chip is being transferred from one base to another in a secured truck.

The truck can move on an infinite two-dimensional plane. Initially, it is located at some point with integer coordinates (H,K)(H, K)(H,K). Unfortunately, this point and the route the truck is going to follow are unknown.

To accomplish his task, David Jones can use two kinds of weapons with the following feedback systems:



Hellfire ― Launched towards a single point (X,Y)(X, Y)(X,Y). Anything lying at this point is instantly destroyed. Let's denote the location of the truck at this point in time by (A,B)(A, B)(A,B). Then, the feedback is one of the following strings:



"O" if X=AX = AX=A and Y=BY = BY=B
"PY" if X>AX \gt AX>A and Y=BY = BY=B
"NY" if X<AX \lt AX<A and Y=BY = BY=B
"XP" if X=AX = AX=A and Y>BY \gt BY>B
"XN" if X=AX = AX=A and Y<BY \lt BY<B
"PP" if X>AX \gt AX>A and Y>BY \gt BY>B
"PN" if X>AX \gt AX>A and Y<BY \lt BY<B
"NP" if X<AX \lt AX<A and Y>BY \gt BY>B
"NN" if X<AX \lt AX<A and Y<BY \lt BY<B



Ring of Fire ― Applied on a rectangular region with a bottom left corner (X1,Y1)(X_1, Y_1)(X1​,Y1​) and a top right corner (X2,Y2)(X_2, Y_2)(X2​,Y2​). Anything lying on the perimeter of the rectangle gets instantly destroyed. Let's denote the location of the truck at this point in time by (A,B)(A, B)(A,B). Then, the feedback is one of the following strings:



"O" if (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) lies on the perimeter of the rectangle
"IN" if (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) lies strictly inside the rectangle
"OUT" if (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) lies outside the rectangle


In addition, if an attack is unsuccessful, the truck moves exactly DDD units in one of the four possible axis-parallel directions, i.e. it moves from its current position (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) to (A+D,B)(A+D, B)(A+D,B) or (A,B+D)(A, B+D)(A,B+D) or (A−D,B)(A-D, B)(A−D,B) or (A,B−D)(A, B-D)(A,B−D) (not necessarily the same direction for all unsuccessful attacks). The value of DDD is fixed and known to be either 000 (the truck does not move) or 111.

Each weapon may be used multiple times and they may be used in any order. However, since using the weapons is costly, you may only make up to QQQ attacks (with both types of weapons in total). Obviously, your task is to destroy the truck, i.e. to receive "O" as the feedback.

Interaction
First, you should read a line containing three space-separated integers TTT, QQQ and DDD. You should solve TTT test cases, each with the same parameters QQQ and DDD. The description of interaction for TTT test cases follows.
For each test case, you may make up to QQQ queries. For each query:

If you want to use a Hellfire attack, print a single line containing three space-separated integers 111, XXX and YYY (−2⋅1018≤X,Y≤2⋅1018-2 \cdot 10^{18} \leq X, Y \leq 2\cdot 10^{18}−2⋅1018≤X,Y≤2⋅1018).
If you want to use a Ring of Fire attack, print a single line containing five space-separated integers 222, X1X_1X1​, Y1Y_1Y1​, X2X_2X2​ and Y2Y_2Y2​ (−2⋅1018≤X1≤X2≤2⋅1018-2 \cdot 10^{18} \leq X_1 \leq X_2 \leq 2 \cdot 10^{18}−2⋅1018≤X1​≤X2​≤2⋅1018 and −2⋅1018≤Y1≤Y2≤2⋅1018-2 \cdot 10^{18} \leq Y_1 \leq Y_2 \leq 2 \cdot 10^{18}−2⋅1018≤Y1​≤Y2​≤2⋅1018).
Then, you should read a single line containing one string. If you ask an invalid query or exceed the query limit, this string is "FAILED" and you should immediately terminate your program to receive the Wrong Answer verdict. Otherwise, this string is the feedback for your attack.


As soon as you receive feedback "O", you should stop making queries and continue solving the remaining test cases.
Note: Some of the test files use a non-adaptive grader, while some use an adaptive grader, where the initial point and the route are not fixed at the start, but the answers to your queries are chosen in such a way that there is always at least one initial point (H,K)(H,K)(H,K) and sequence of moves which are consistent with all answers.

**Don't forget to flush the output after printing each line!**
constraints: All coordinates used in queries must satisfy -2·10^18 ≤ X,Y ≤ 2·10^18
The truck moves at most D units after each unsuccessful attack
content.inputFormat: The first line contains three space‑separated integers T, Q and D (1 ≤ T ≤ 2048, Q ≤ 72, D∈{0,1}). Then for each of the T test cases you may interact with the grader using up to Q queries. Each query is either a Hellfire attack (type 1) or a Ring of Fire attack (type 2) as described in the statement. After printing a query you must read a single string feedback from the grader ("O", "PY", "NY", "XP", "XN", "PP", "PN", "NP", "NN", "IN", "OUT" or "FAILED"). The interaction ends for a test case as soon as you receive feedback "O".
content.outputFormat: There is no conventional output. The program must print queries exactly in the format required by the interactive protocol and flush after each line. When the feedback "O" is received for a test case the program proceeds to the next test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The interactive transcript from the statement is omitted because offline judges cannot simulate interaction. The program would read T=1, Q=64, D=1 and then start querying the grader as described.

solution.approach:
The goal is to locate the unknown integer point (A,B) using at most Q directional feedbacks. Each Hellfire query gives the relative quadrant of the truck, which is equivalent to a 2‑D binary search step. By repeatedly halving the search rectangle with Hellfire we can shrink the possible region to a single point in O(log |coordinate|) steps (≤ 60 for the given limits). When D=1 the truck may move after a failed query; however the movement is limited to one unit in a cardinal direction, so we can keep a margin of one around the current search rectangle. A robust strategy is:
1. Maintain a rectangle [Lx,Rx]×[Ly,Ry] that certainly contains the truck.
2. Choose the centre (mx,my) of the rectangle and fire a Hellfire at (mx,my).
3. From the feedback we shrink the rectangle to the corresponding quadrant (or side) and then enlarge it by D in all four directions to compensate for a possible move.
4. Repeat until the rectangle collapses to a single cell; fire a final Hellfire at that cell – the feedback will be "O".
The same idea works with Ring of Fire to cut off large empty areas, but Hellfire alone is sufficient for the given limits. The algorithm uses at most 64 queries, well within the limits for both D=0 and D=1.
The implementation follows the interactive protocol, flushes after each query and stops after receiving "O" for a test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q) per test case (each query is O(1)). Overall O(T·Q).
